seoTitle: How to Create an Organizational Chart in Power BI
metaDescription: Build a dynamic org chart in Power BI using employee and manager ID relationships, custom visuals from AppSource, and department slicer filters.
intro: Static drawing tools make it hard to keep an organizational chart current as reporting lines change. Power BI can turn a structured employee database into a dynamic, interactive hierarchy chart using parent-child relationships. This guide covers structuring your data and bringing in a custom visual to render the chart.

steps.heading: Structure your employee data
steps.body: Assign clear Employee ID and Manager ID columns in your source data so Power BI can automatically form logical tree paths between reporting lines.

steps.heading: Import a custom visual from AppSource
steps.body: Browse the AppSource marketplace for a certified organizational chart visual designed to render parent-child hierarchies.

steps.heading: Map the hierarchy fields
steps.body: Connect the Employee ID and Manager ID fields to the visual's expected inputs so it can build the reporting structure correctly.

steps.heading: Adjust layout scale and styling
steps.body: Set custom layout scale factors and styling options so the chart remains readable even with a large number of employees.

steps.heading: Add department slicers
steps.body: Configure slicer toggles for department filtering so viewers can narrow the chart down to a specific team or division.

faq.question: Can Power BI build an org chart without a custom visual?
faq.answer: Native parent-child DAX functions like PATH and PATHITEM can support hierarchy logic, but a certified marketplace visual is generally needed to actually render the visual tree structure.

faq.question: What data do I need to build an org chart?
faq.answer: At minimum, you need an Employee ID column and a corresponding Manager ID column that maps each employee to their direct manager.

faq.question: Will the chart update automatically when reporting lines change?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's built from live data, updating the underlying Manager ID values and refreshing the report updates the chart structure.

faq.question: Can I filter the chart by department?
faq.answer: Yes, adding slicers connected to a department field lets viewers narrow the chart down to specific teams.
